questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: Neha wants to be ______ Music therapist.
questions.explanation: 'Music' starts with the consonant sound /m/, so we use 'a'.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: My uncle is ______ accountant.
questions.explanation: 'Accountant' starts with the vowel sound /a/, so we use 'an'.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: ______ Philosophy is not usually taught in state schools.
questions.explanation: No article is used before uncountable abstract nouns like 'Philosophy' when used in a general sense.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: Yesterday ______ European called at my office.
questions.explanation: 'European' starts with the consonant sound /j/ (as in 'you'), so we use 'a', not 'an'.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: ______ Ganges is a sacred river.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' before the names of rivers. The Ganges, the Nile, the Amazon, etc.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: Copper is ______ useful metal.
questions.explanation: 'Useful' starts with the consonant sound /j/ (as in 'you'), so we use 'a', not 'an'.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: We are going to a barbeque with ______ Simpsons.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' before a family name in the plural to refer to the entire family. The Simpsons = the Simpson family.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: She starts work on ______ Monday next week.
questions.explanation: No article is used before days of the week when referring to a specific upcoming day. 'Monday next week' is already specific.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: Close all ______ doors and windows when you leave.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' when the noun is specific and understood by both speaker and listener. The doors and windows of the room being discussed are known.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with a, an, the, or X: Rohith and Arjun are ______ best bowlers in the team.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' before superlatives. 'Best' is a superlative adjective.

questions.question: Fill in the blanks with a, an, the, or X: ______ MBBS degree is essential for ______ person to become a doctor.
questions.explanation: 'MBBS' is pronounced starting with the vowel sound /e/ ('em-bee-bee-ess'), so we use 'an'. 'Person' starts with the consonant sound /p/, so we use 'a'.

questions.question: Fill in the blanks with a, an, the, or X: ______ most beautiful scene I admired was one of ______ setting sun.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' before a superlative ('most beautiful'). We also use 'the' before 'setting sun' because there is only one sun, making it unique.
